steps.title: Test which point satisfies both inequalities
steps.content: A solution must satisfy both: $y \le x + 7$ AND $y \ge -2x - 1$. Substitute each choice into both inequalities.

steps.title: Test the other choices through C
steps.content:
• Choice A — $\left(-14,0\right)$: $0 \le -14+7$ becomes $0 \le -7$, which is false.
  • Choice B — $\left(0,-14\right)$: $-14 \le 0+7$ is true, but $-14 \ge -2(0)-1$ becomes $-14 \ge -1$, which is false.
  • Choice C — $\left(0,14\right)$: $14 \le 0+7$ becomes $14 \le 7$, which is false.

steps.title: Testing the correct answer (14, 0)
steps.content: First inequality: $0 \le 14 + 7 = 21$? Yes. Second inequality: $0 \ge -2(14) - 1 = -28 - 1 = -29$? Yes. Both inequalities are satisfied. D is correct.
